methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals institutional capacity-building in Richmond Metro, but the concentration of university-led AI education initiatives in urban centers risks leaving non-metro regions dependent on asynchronous, platform-delivered training rather than peer networks and deep learning communities.

signals.title: ‘Shaping our future’: Company powering AI data centers invests $50+ million in Henrico, adding 200 jobs - WWBT
signals.signal_reading: A $50M data center infrastructure investment in Henrico adds 200 jobs but signals concentrated physical AI infrastructure rather than distributed capability—capital flows to metro hubs while workforce development questions remain about whether these roles require deep AI fluency or shallow operational skills.

signals.title: RC-RV partners with ed2go to launch innovative, on-demand workforce training programs - Roanoke College
signals.signal_reading: Roanoke College's partnership with ed2go for on-demand workforce training represents platform-delivered, asynchronous AI education reaching beyond metro areas—potentially enabling distributed but shallow adoption rather than peer-driven depth, fitting the 'Software Does It For You' pathway for rural/small metro regions.

signals.title: Roanoke College president: Virginia’s future workforce depends on fixing the path from college to career
signals.signal_reading: Roanoke College's president identifies AI displacing entry-level tasks that build workforce experience, threatening the college-to-career pipeline especially in regions like Southwest and Southside where alternatives to traditional credential pathways are already limited—signals risk of neither depth nor distribution materializing outside metro centers.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: Axios reporting on AI pressure facing Richmond's young workforce indicates displacement anxiety without corresponding signals of systematic upskilling or peer learning networks—suggests shallow platform exposure without deep capability development, even in a growing metro hub.
